calendar.matters:
Petition For: (1) Breach Of Trust; (2) Compelling Trustee To Return Trust Property To Trust; (3) Compelling Distribution From The Trust; (4) Compellin
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Off Calendar

Petition For: (1) Breach Of Trust; (2) Compelling Trustee To Return Trust Property To Trust; (3) Compelling Distribution From The Trust; (4) Compellin
Off Calendar Sep-16-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rebecca Quitman appeared by telephone for Alanna Reed (Petitioner).
Ross Madden appeared by telephone for Bernylda Reed-Renteria (Respondent).

The Court asked if there have been any new developments since filing of the status report by Mr. Madden. Ms. Quitman stated that they settled the matter this morning and asked the court if it would retain jurisdiction over performance of the settlement terms under CCP 664.6 and set an order to show cause regarding status of the settlement for the end of January 2121. The Petition for Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to January 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of the settlement. A joint status report is to be filed one week prior.
Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Court Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: L. Maloney. Not reported.

Petition For: (1) Breach Of Trust; (2) Compelling Trustee To Return Trust Property To Trust; (3) Compelling Distribution From The Trust; (4) Compellin
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Sep-16-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For: (1) Breach Of Trust; (2) Compelling Trustee To Return Trust Property To Trust; (3) Compelling Distribution From The Trust; (4) Compellin
Off Calendar Feb-19-2020 Continued To Jun-24-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Yasha Bronshteyn for Alanna Reed (Petitioner)
Ross Madden for and with Bernylda Reed-Renteria (Respondent).

Mr. Bronshteyn stated that this is a 14-year trust administration, that they mediated at the end of last Summer/Fall, that they ultimately needed to file this petition, that he needs to do some discovery and that he is ready to mediate, or if not, get the matter set for trial. Mr. Madden stated that they spoke about a buyout and suggested doing mediation before doing discovery. The Court advised Counsel that it will not set a trial date until the Parties are ready and spoke of the procedure for sending the Parties out to the Trial Department. The Petition for Breach of Trust, etc. is continued to June 24, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court anticipates that the Parties will participate in private mediation. The Parties are to file a report as to the status of mediation one week before the hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Singh. Not reported.
